title: Creative Director [210919]

This dynamic agency is dedicated to crafting compelling narratives and impactful strategies that resonate deeply with audiences, driving significant results for its diverse portfolio of clients. With a collaborative spirit and a commitment to excellence, they empower organizations to achieve their full potential through strategic creative vision and execution. Are you a visionary leader with a passion for shaping compelling brand experiences and inspiring creative teams? This is an exceptional opportunity to make a profound impact, driving the creative direction for a diverse portfolio of clients and influencing the strategic vision of an entire agency. You will be at the forefront of developing groundbreaking campaigns, mentoring top talent, and forging deep, trusting relationships that elevate both our clients and our agency to new heights. Join us and unleash your full creative and leadership potential! As a pivotal member of the leadership team, you will establish the creative vision for engagements of all scales, inspiring interdisciplinary teams of writers, art directors, and designers to produce their best work. Your expertise will be crucial in fostering collaborative relationships across strategy, digital, accounts, media, and research, ensuring the delivery of innovative and high-quality creative solutions. Lead teams of designers, writers, and producers in developing resonant and inspiring concepts rooted in strategic insights. Direct the creation of both digital and traditional deliverables, including advertising, style guides, print collateral, and websites. Oversee, review, and manage all creative deliverables, coordinating presentations with client-facing teams. Lead client meetings and presentations, providing input for client reports and project deliverables. Provide thoughtful and timely responses to client inquiries and requests. Participate in discovery sessions and meetings related to brand and creative development. Offer strategic expertise throughout projects, including input on scope, budgets, and deliverables. Manage resource allocation, determining creative team composition based on project needs. Develop or approve creative project timelines, ensuring high-quality, on-time, and on-budget delivery. **Integrated Creative Direction:** Collaborate with User Experience and Development teams to create diverse website experiences, directing art and editorial for storytelling and content. Partner with Video teams and external partners to bring concepts to life, including script development, storyboarding, art/photography direction, and post-production reviews. Work with Media Strategy to design and deliver impactful, high-performing campaigns, applying best practices for chosen mediums. Collaborate with the Research team by providing input on qualitative and quantitative creative testing survey instruments. **Business Development & Thought Leadership:** Contribute content related to brand development and creative strategy for proposals and sales presentations. Participate in sales presentations to prospective clients. Write newsletter and blog articles to position the company as an industry thought leader. Develop and maintain relationships with clients and industry professionals to identify potential leads and uphold the company’s reputation. Assist in developing case studies of project work for the company website and proposals. **Management & Mentorship:** Cultivate an equitable, inclusive, and psychologically safe work environment where all employees can thrive. Attend leadership-level meetings to ensure alignment with agency policy and strateg

title: Creative Director, Yahoo Search & Yahoo Mail

With its beautiful design and lightning fast speed, Yahoo Mail makes reading, organizing, and sending emails easier than ever. A Little About Us The Yahoo Creative Marketing Studio is a centralized shared services organization that powers the visual and verbal identity of Yahoo’s iconic portfolio. From News and Finance to Sports, Mail, and Search, we create integrated marketing campaigns that drive growth and brand affinity globally. We sit at the intersection of utility and delight, operating as a high-energy hub of strategists, designers, and storytellers. Position Summary As the Creative Director dedicated to the Yahoo Search and Mail brands, you will define the overarching creative vision and drive the marketing strategies that elevate these essential products within our shared services model. Reporting to the Head of Marketing Studio, you will act as a strategic individual contributor, partnering closely with product and marketing leadership to move from planning to high-impact execution. You are a player-coach: a high-level strategic thinker with exceptional creative taste who remains a hands-on designer and storyteller when the work demands it. You will lead by influence, ensuring that our creative output—both visual and verbal—is fresh, on-brand, and highly optimized through modern, AI-augmented workflows. Key Responsibilities Creative Vision & Architecture: Define and drive the multi-year creative strategy and visual systems for Yahoo Search and Yahoo Mail, establishing scalable creative frameworks that align campaign execution with long-term brand objectives. Campaign Orchestration & Delivery: Oversee the visual and conceptual direction of high-impact, multi-channel campaigns across digital display, social media, video storytelling, and interactive experiences, maintaining the ultimate backstop for creative quality. Cross-Functional & Strategic Partnering: Partner closely with Product Marketing, Product Design, and Engineering to craft actionable, strategic creative briefs. Represent design and branding at the highest strategic levels to build cross-functional consensus. Intelligent Campaign Optimization: Drive campaign pipeline efficiency by utilizing Asana AI to automate administrative workflows, orchestrate squad tasks, and streamline review handoffs. Mentorship & Practice Leadership: Multiply the capability of the design organization by mentoring and empowering senior designers (IC4/IC5), raising the bar for craftsmanship, professional maturity, and creative output. Responsible Innovation: Stay at the forefront of emerging technologies and industry shifts, formulating ethical guidelines for how the creative team leverages AI platforms to maintain brand integrity and avoid market redundancy. Qualifications 10+ years of creative leadership experience in digital media, advertising, or consumer tech, with a proven history of shaping high-level brand visions. A portfolio showing exceptional visual taste, multi-channel campaign leadership, and master-level execution across video, digital, and upper-funnel brand initiatives. Deep experience establishing strategic design and storytelling systems for complex consumer tech products (ideally communication or utility tools). Demonstrated ability to validate, refine, and provide creative governance over AI-generated outputs, ensuring high standards of brand-safety, originality, and visual polish. Proven experience driving cross-team alignment on complex projects, negotiating through conflicting stakeholder feedback to establish clear creative pathways. Skilled in modern design and collaboration environments (Figma, Adobe Creative Suite). Exceptional communication and narrative skills, with the ability to articulate deep strategic rationale behind creative choices to senior business leaders. Bachelor’s degree

title: Senior Vice President, Creative Strategy

They bring a sharp strategic mind, a strong point of view, and the ability to work fluidly across accounts — concepting, directing, and delivering work that earns attention and drives results. This role is client-facing, execution-oriented, and built for someone who loves the work itself. Key Responsibilities Client Creative Strategy Serve as the senior creative strategy lead across a portfolio of client accounts, developing insight-driven creative strategies that align with client business objectives and communications goals. Translate client briefs, audience insights, and market research into clear, compelling creative platforms that guide campaign development. Lead creative strategy sessions with clients, presenting ideas and recommendations with confidence and clarity. Act as a trusted creative partner to senior client stakeholders, providing strategic counsel that strengthens relationships and elevates the quality of the work. Ensure creative strategies are grounded in data and audience understanding, while leaving meaningful room for originality and creative ambition. Campaign Development & Execution Lead the conceptual development of integrated campaigns across paid, earned, shared, and owned channels — from initial brief through to final delivery. Work closely with Highwire's creative, digital, content, and communications teams to bring campaign ideas to life cohesively and consistently across all touchpoints. Direct the development of creative deliverables — including visual concepts, messaging frameworks, campaign narratives, and content — ensuring every output meets the highest standard of quality. Oversee multiple campaign workstreams simultaneously, maintaining creative integrity and strategic alignment across all accounts. Ensure all work delivered to clients is polished, on-brief, and ready to perform — with zero tolerance for errors or misalignment. New Business & Pitch Support Develop and present creative strategy concepts for new business pitches, crafting ideas that are bold, insight-led, and clearly differentiated. Collaborate closely with Highwire's strategy and business development teams to ensure pitch creative is tightly aligned with prospective client goals and business context. Bring a senior, compelling voice to pitch presentations — articulating a clear creative vision that inspires confidence and wins business. Creative Execution Across Accounts Provide senior-level creative direction and feedback on work across the agency's client portfolio throughout the engagement. Partner with account leads to understand client priorities and ensure creative strategy is aligned with broader campaign and communications planning. Bring fresh thinking, cultural awareness, and creative ambition to every brief — challenging the team to reach for ideas that are genuinely original and impactful. Stay attuned to trends across industries, platforms, and culture, applying relevant insights to client work in meaningful and timely ways. Success Factors 12+ years of creative strategy experience, with significant time in senior creative roles within a top-tier agency or in-house environment. An exceptional portfolio demonstrating strategic, cross-channel creative thinking with proven business impact across multiple industries. title: Executive Creative Director
description: Join Our Spiro·Verse THIS IS A REMOTE OPPORTUNITY IN THE US. Company Overview This is Spiro. A strategically-led, creatively-driven, leading global experiential agency. We exist to cultivate powerful brand connections through the channel of experiential. We believe strong global communities are empowered by diversity, inclusion & belonging. Where data-informed creative meets advanced digital solutions, we boldly go where no experience has gone before. As Spiro nauts, we are born storytellers & story makers; fearless dreamers of the “what if;" and pioneers of innovation in our craft. Together, we launch brands into their experiential orbit through the power of Brand Gravity. Learn more about who we are here! Position Overview Do you consider yourself one heck of a storyteller? Do you thrive on shaping not just ideas, but entire creative organizations? Are you as passionate about building teams and culture as you are about delivering breakthrough work? Do you know how to balance bold creative ambition with real business impact? Then we want to talk to you! Ensure alignment between creative vision, business objectives, operational realities and client expectations. Partner with Strategy, Production, Sales and Marketing leadership to shape integrated, experience-led solutions. Oversee the conceptual development of large-scale, high-impact programs and initiatives. Provide high-level creative direction and final approval on key work. Lead and evolve creative processes, frameworks and standards to improve efficiency and output quality. Champion innovation — driving the adoption of new technologies, tools and approaches to elevate creative thinking and execution. Build and nurture a culture of creativity, collaboration and continuous growth across the global team. Guide resource allocation and team structure to support business needs and creative excellence. Function as a creative stimulus. Support department head the EVP, ECD, as requested, for feedback on creative team that work most closely with you on account related work. As the head of the animation team, you will collaborate with a diverse group of creative and production professionals, shaping the foundation and processes for the studio’s first anime series. This position requires a proactive leader with a passion for anime, strong communication skills, and the ability to build scalable workflows for distributed teams. title: Senior Vice President of Marketing
description: About The Role The Senior Vice President (SVP) of Marketing will serve as the architect of Precision AQ's marketing vision, orchestrating strategies that drive exceptional business growth, amplify brand authority, and generate impactful, high-quality leads. This role demands a dynamic and visionary leader with a proven history of creating transformative marketing strategies, achieving measurable outcomes, and fostering cross-functional alignment across diverse teams. The ideal candidate brings deep expertise in marketing both services and products while excelling in the management of digital and event-driven marketing initiatives. Essential Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Define and champion an innovative marketing vision and roadmap to position Precision as a leader in the industry Craft and execute an integrated, data-driven marketing strategy to enhance lead generation, and brand visibility. Stay ahead of industry trends, competitive activities, and emerging market opportunities to refine and future-proof Precision's marketing strategy. Partner closely with the business unit and other senior leaders to ensure alignment between marketing efforts and overall business objectives. Define clear metrics to evaluate marketing performance and support data-driven decision-making. Oversee and strategically manage the marketing budget to ensure effective allocation of resources for maximum impact. Events Marketing Develop and implement a events-based awareness and lead gen strategy for conferences, webinars, and industry events to maximize Precision’s visibility and influence. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to secure Precision’s participation in high-profile sponsorships, speaking engagements, panels, and industry discussions. Leverage event analytics to measure success, gather feedback, and continuously optimize event strategies for maximum impact. Thought Leadership & Public Relations Establish Precision as an authoritative voice in industry through strategic thought leadership initiatives. Oversee the strategy for high-value thought leadership content, such as white papers, case studies, blogs, and industry reports. Guide PR agency with intel to develop and execute a PR strategy to promote key achievements, partnerships, experts, and innovations through media outreach, press releases, and news features. Digital Marketing Design strategy and guide team to develop and execute high-impact marketing campaigns informed by market insights, customer feedback, and business priorities. Collaborate with internal teams to leverage martech tools such as HubSpot, Salesforce, Google Analytics, SEMrush, and Google Ads for superior campaign performance. Lead website optimization efforts to ensure alignment with Precision's branding and business goals. Qualifications Required: 10+ years of leadership experience in senior marketing roles, with a track record of success at the SVP or VP level. Demonstrated success in the pharmaceutical, healthcare consulting, or related industries. Expertise in marketing data, software, and technology products across digital and traditional channels. Strong writing abilities. Preferred Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field, a plus Experience driving marketing strategies for data-driven products or SaaS solutions. Proficiency in marketing platforms such as SEMrush, HubSpot, Google Ads, and Google Analytics. Key Skills Visionary leadership with a demonstrated ability to inspire and lead high-performing teams. Exceptional storytelling and communication skills to craft compelling brand narratives. Advanced analytical and problem-solving skills to inform and refine strategic decisions. Proficiency in designing and executing innovative, results-driven marketing campaigns. Superior project management capabilities, adept at managing competing priorities under tight deadlines. A strategic mindset focused on delivering measurable and sustainable business outcomes. Compens
score_reason: Senior title and marketing leadership scope fit, but no mention of remote work, creative DNA is weak (B2B services/lead gen focus), and location/work arrangement not specified—likely on-site or hybrid.

title: Director, Creative Strategy
description: At Viral Nation, we specialize in building social-first ecosystems for brands to connect with the modern consumer journey. Our integrated solutions align strategy, talent, media, and technology with culturally relevant creativity to scale the world’s fastest-growing digital brands. Viral Nation offers a fluid, creative, and growth-oriented environment that will support your ambitions to apply your talents in an open, collaborative, and fast-paced culture. Our ability to stay at the forefront of the industry has fuelled our success and will guide us in paving the path forward. We’re driven to push boundaries and think beyond today to deliver strategies, and we’re just getting started. While we continuously exceed our goals, we need your help – our success is only as great as our people. Strong performance leads to high expectations, and we must keep raising the bar! This role serves as a senior creative strategy leader responsible for ensuring the quality, clarity, and performance relevance of social-first creative output across priority client work. They own the end-to-end integrity of creative strategy, ensuring strong strategic thinking informs every concept, campaign idea, and recommendation. Acting as the final strategic pass on key deliverables, they guide teams toward platform-native ideas grounded in cultural insights, performance data, and client objectives. In close partnership with Strategy, Business Intelligence, and cross-functional teams, they translate insights and trends into clear creative opportunities that drive impact. As an anchor leader within the division, they strengthen collaboration across teams, elevate creative standards, and help shape strategic narratives for client engagements, new business, and the continued evolution of Viral Nation’s creative approach. What You'll Do Here: Own the end-to-end quality of creative strategy output, ensuring strategic thinking is the through-line behind every creative idea that gets delivered. Be the accountable “final pass” for creative strategy deliverables across priority work, with a bias toward clarity, platform-native thinking, and performance relevance. Drive the development of social-first creative concepts, campaign ideas, and strategic recommendations aligned with client objectives. Partner with Strategy and Business Intelligence to translate cultural, platform, and performance signals into clear creative opportunity areas. Operate as an anchor leader within the division, sitting across multiple opportunities/clients and guiding the pod’s strategic-to-creative output. Use performance data and post-campaign insights to refine creative strategy frameworks, ensuring learnings translate into stronger future concepts and platform approaches Provide direct leadership, including improving output quality, sharpening strategic rigor, and raising the standard of social-first thinking. Serve as the primary point of alignment for senior creative partners for cohesion, quality control, and strategic consistency. Shape and strengthen strategic narratives, pitches, and creative storytelling for new business and client engagements. Support and, when required, lead client presentations, translating strategy into clear creative direction and actionable next steps. Advise on creative direction during content development/production, ensuring alignment with platform trends and performance insights. Develop and evolve repeatable creative strategy frameworks (platform playbooks, concepting structures, signal-to-idea methodologies) that elevate consistency and quality across teams. Translate cultural trends, platform updates, and performance insights into actionable creative guidance for clients and internal teams. Contribute hands-on to ideation and concept development when needed to unblock teams or elevate creative thinking. title: Director of Marketing
description: Position: Director of Marketing The Opportunity: The creator economy runs on people doing everything themselves: shoot, edit, write, design, post, repeat, until they burn out. Made exists to give them their time and their craft back. We're building the creative OS for creators, and we're looking for the marketer who's going to put it on the map. Here's the part most early roles can't offer: this is a ground-floor seat at a company that's already real. Made is a brand-new product, but it's built by RHEI, which has spent two decades working with the biggest names in media (Sony Pictures, Universal, Paramount) and partners with thousands of creators today. So you get the rare combination: the blank canvas and upside of an early product, with the network, credibility, and backing of a company that's lived in the creator world for 20 years. About RHEI: RHEI is a creator economy company advancing the industry by helping creators, media companies and brands find success through digital content. We provide end-to-end solutions to help creators and media companies grow their audiences and revenue, while helping brands connect to hard-to-reach digital fans. Our proprietary technologies leverage generative AI, machine learning, digital signal processing and big data to power our platform and ecosystem, and we are the largest multi-vertical video publisher in the world, reaching tens of billions of monthly views and over 600 million monthly uniques. About Made: Made gives creators a full creative team without the overhead: clip and edit video, design thumbnails, write scripts and posts, engage with the community and publish everywhere their audience lives. Our beliefs are simple: 1. AI should elevate human creativity, not try to replace it 2. No creator should burn out doing the work of an entire studio alone. About the role: You'll own how Made reaches creators, earns their trust, and turns them into people who can't imagine creating without it. This is a hands-on, player-coach role: you do the work yourself now, and build the team as the momentum proves you right. As RHEI is a high growth company, you should enjoy working in an entrepreneurial, high change environment. RHEI has a remote work model which offers the best work life balance. Key Responsibilities: Own Made's marketing end to end: positioning, brand, website, social, and the story we tell the world. Find the wedge: the one thing creators get genuinely excited about and will change their routine for. Run a creator-first, organic motion: demos and tutorials as marketing, content-led growth, real community presence. We earn attention, we don't buy it with paid blitzes. Turn RHEI's creator relationships into design partners, proof, and word-of-mouth. Build your team: a content and social creator and a designer to start, more as you prove what works. Work shoulder-to-shoulder with product positioning, onboarding, and what makes creators stick. Other duties as assigned Key Requirements: 6+ years in marketing, with the range to set the strategy and ship the work yourself. You're of the creator economy: you've worked at a creator tool, platform, creator-commerce, or creator-facing brand, and you get how creators actually discover and adopt new things. A builder, not a delegator. You'll personally write the homepage, cut the demo, and run the channels until there's a team to hand them to. A track record taking marketing from 0-to-1 at an early-stage company. Bonus points You've built a marketing function from scratch before. You're a creator yourself, or you have real standing and relationships in the creator world.
score_reason: Director of Marketing is mid-level, not senior creative leadership; no mention of remote work, compensation, or creative scope beyond marketing execution.

title: Art Director/Creative Director
description: About BiOptimizers BiOptimizers exist to help people go from baseline health to peak biological performance. We develop science-backed supplements and wellness tools that deliver measurable results. As a remote-first company, our globally distributed team focuses on clarity, autonomy, and operational excellence. Every role is designed to support the health and performance of both our customers and our organization. As BiOptimizers' Art Director/Creative Director , you will define and elevate how our brand shows up across every touchpoint, from campaigns and brand activations to product storytelling and real-world experiences. You're a hands-on leader who brings both strategic vision and a roll-up-your-sleeves attitude to execute creative work that grows our brand and drives sales across retail, Amazon, and DTC channels. Reporting directly to the Chief Marketing Officer (CMO), you'll own visual direction and execution across all channels, partnering closely with Brand, Social, Influencer, and Production to bring ideas to life. You'll also lead, mentor, and grow a team of designers who report directly to you. This is a role for someone who can balance creative instinct with strategic clarity, who isn't afraid to get into the details alongside their team, and who thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment. This is a fully remote position, with team members based in various locations and time zones. You must have the ability to travel for events and shoots, which are primarily in the Southwest U.S. Key Responsibilities Lead, manage, and mentor the full design team, fostering a collaborative, high-output creative culture while remaining actively involved in day-to-day creative execution. Develop and lead creative concepts for campaigns, product launches, content shoots, and brand activations across DTC, Amazon, and retail channels. Drive retail creative execution including on-shelf displays, in-store merchandising, point-of-sale materials, and retail display programs, ensuring the brand shows up powerfully at the shelf. Oversee and contribute to Amazon creative assets, including A+ content, brand storefronts, and product detail page visuals, optimized for both conversion and brand consistency. Translate complex, science-backed messaging into clear, compelling visual storytelling across all channels and formats. Direct photo and video shoots, ensuring creative vision translates into high-quality, high-performing assets. Maintain and evolve brand guidelines to ensure consistency across all outputs, from digital to physical. Partner with Brand, Social, and Influencer teams to align on business and campaign goals. Collaborate with external agencies, freelancers, and production partners to scale creative output. Required Qualifications 7 - 10+ years of experience in art direction or visual design within consumer, wellness, performance, or lifestyle brands, with at least 3 years in an Art Director or senior creative leadership role. Proven experience managing and leading a design team, with the ability to both direct at a high level and personally contribute to creative execution when needed. Bachelor's degree in Art, Graphic Design, Marketing, or a related field. Strong portfolio demonstrating concept-driven campaigns and multi-channel execution, with clear examples spanning digital (DTC, Amazon, social) and retail/physical environments (displays, merchandising, in-store). Deep experience in retail creative, including on-shelf displays, endcap displays, point-of-purchase materials, and retail merchandising systems. Demonstrated experience with DTC and Amazon creative, including Amazon A+ content, storefronts, and performance-oriented digital assets. Highly skilled with industry-standard creative tools including Adobe Creative Cloud (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and Figma. Experience translating technical or science-based concepts into accessible, engaging creative work. Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the
score_reason: Senior creative leadership (Art Director/Creative Director reporting to CMO), fully remote, DTC/retail/Amazon experience aligns with Travis's integrated campaign background, though no explicit USD salary and travel requirement is minor friction.

title: Game Director
description: The Role We are working with a small, ambitious studio working on a new action adventure title being built in UE5. This studio is searching for a Game Director who will be responsible for defining and elevating the vision of their project. This studio is offering completely remote work and no relocation is required here. In this role you will be the guardian of the game's creative vision. You will be a hands-on creative leader who knows exactly what good feels like. Your primary focus will be delivering a gameplay loop centered around world-class combat and fluid, expressive traversal. You will align design, art, audio, and engineering to ensure every punch, leap, swing, and dash feels unforgettable. Key Responsibilities Own the Creative Vision: Establish, articulate, and maintain the "North Star" vision for the game from pre-production through to launch and live operations. Ensure all departments are aligned and inspired by this vision. Mastery of Feel (Combat & Traversal): Directly oversee the core gameplay loop. You will champion the feel, pacing, and mechanics of character movement, environmental navigation, and combat systems, pushing the team to achieve a world-class standard of execution. Direct Multi-Disciplinary Leadership: Lead and mentor the design, art and technical leads. You will foster a culture of rapid iteration, psychological safety, and creative excellence. UE5 Product Guardrails: Partner with Technical Leads to leverage the cutting-edge capabilities of Unreal Engine 5 (e.g., Motion Matching, Enhanced Input, Control Rig) to push the boundaries of animation fidelity and systemic gameplay. Stakeholder Alignment: Act as the primary creative voice to studio leadership. You will clearly communicate product goals, creative milestones, and strategic pivots when necessary. If you have at least one shipped, critically acclaimed action-adventure, character-action, or combat game where you held a primary leadership role (Game Director, Creative Director, or Game Design Director) from conception to shipping, then please do get in touch with me as this could be a strong option for you to consider. Please send a copy of your CV to michael@levelupgaming.io and/or apply here and I will get back to you as quickly as possible.
score_reason: Senior creative leadership in gaming with remote flexibility, but lacks advertising/brand DNA and compensation details make it hard to assess fit against Travis's core expertise.

title: Head of Creative Services

title: Creative Director

title: Creative Strategist
description: As this brand is scaling and spending 7 figures on Meta Ads monthly, creative performance is the single biggest growth lever for them. Our client is looking for a Creative Strategist who bridges the gap between raw performance data and the psychology of why people click, watch, and buy. You will lead a dedicated pod of creatives with a clear mission: systematically transform data into ads that actually scale. WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU True ownership : You have full autonomy to propose, prioritize, and approve the creative pipeline from start to finish. Clear career path : Both Senior Creative Strategist and Head of Creative seats are open; the brand prefers to promote high performers as they scale toward 9 figures. Compensation : Competitive salary, plus a performance bonus structure ranging from 30% to 50% of your base. Comprehensive benefits : Health, dental, and vision insurance, plus 2 weeks of PTO and major company holidays. Flexibility : Work 100% remotely with one flex day per quarter to recharge whenever you desire. WHAT YOU’LL OWN Develop and execute a cross-channel strategy (Meta, TikTok, AppLovin) that directly increases MER and allows for a 50% increase in ad spend. Fuel the growth engine by producing 20+ unique, high-quality concepts every week through the delivery of 4 videos and 3 static briefs daily. Lead a team of 4 Video Editors and 1 Graphic Designer to achieve same-day feedback loops, ensuring zero friction in the transition from brief to live asset. Convert complex data from Ads Manager and Motion into actionable creative iterations, moving from "opinion-based" creative to "winning-pattern" execution. Produce high-converting ad scripts and short-form copy that leverage VSL and UGC formats to lower CPAs and stabilize top-of-funnel performance. Build and refine creative testing frameworks that eliminate waste by identifying "winners" early and scaling them systematically across the account. WHO YOU ARE You have a verifiable track record in the Facebook Ads Library, ideally with 20+ active ads for marketing-first consumer brands. You are comfortable navigating Ads Manager to analyze Hook CTR, Hold Rate, and CPA, but you can also write a compelling 5-minute ad script. You know how to get the best out of editors and designers, giving clear, direct feedback that raises the creative bar. You have deep experience in problem-solution messaging and direct-response copywriting - you can diagnose why a script failed, not just that it did. You are a self-driver who thrives in a high-accountability environment and prefers results over conceptual "brand" fluff.
score_reason: Remote + growth trajectory is strong, but performance marketing/DTC creative (Meta/TikTok volume play) misaligns with Travis's integrated campaign DNA and senior leadership scope.

title: Chief Marketing Officer
description: Descripción de la empresa PNE AG Americas es una empresa del sector de energías renovables centrada en el desarrollo, financiación, construcción y operación de proyectos sostenibles. Trabajamos con equipos internacionales y multidisciplinares para impulsar soluciones energéticas innovadoras en diferentes mercados. Nuestra cultura se basa en la colaboración, la responsabilidad y el compromiso con la transición energética. Ofrecemos un entorno dinámico donde podrás asumir responsabilidades reales y contribuir al crecimiento de la empresa en la región de América. Apostamos por el desarrollo profesional continuo y por proyectos con impacto positivo a largo plazo. Descripción del puesto Como Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) en PNE AG Americas liderarás la estrategia de marketing para la región, asegurando la alineación con los objetivos globales de la empresa. En tu día a día, diseñarás y ejecutarás planes de marketing integrados, gestionarás el presupuesto del área y coordinarás campañas multicanal orientadas a la generación de demanda y posicionamiento de marca. Supervisarás estudios de mercado para identificar oportunidades de crecimiento, tendencias del sector y necesidades de clientes e inversores. También te encargarás de las relaciones públicas y la comunicación corporativa, trabajando de cerca con equipos de ventas, desarrollo de negocio y dirección general. Este es un puesto a tiempo completo y en modalidad remota, con colaboración constante con equipos internacionales y diferentes zonas horarias. Requisitos Aptitudes sólidas en planificación de mercado y gestión de marketing, con experiencia diseñando e implementando estrategias de marca y go-to-market. Experiencia en investigación de mercado y análisis de datos para tomar decisiones basadas en información cuantitativa y cualitativa. Conocimientos en relaciones públicas y comunicación, incluyendo gestión de medios, posicionamiento corporativo y organización de eventos. Aptitudes comerciales en coordinación con ventas, orientadas a la generación de leads, apoyo a desarrollo de negocio y optimización del funnel comercial. Experiencia previa en puestos de dirección de marketing o C-level, preferiblemente en energías renovables, infraestructuras, tecnología o sectores B2B. Titulación universitaria en Marketing, ADE, Comunicación, Economía o similar; se valorarán estudios de posgrado (MBA, máster en marketing estratégico o digital). Capacidad para liderar equipos distribuidos, gestionar proyectos complejos y trabajar de forma colaborativa en entornos internacionales. Nivel alto de inglés hablado y escrito; se valorará positivamente el conocimiento de otros idiomas. Orientación a resultados, pensamiento estratégico, habilidades analíticas y excelente comunicación escrita y oral.
score_reason: Senior CMO title and remote work hit, but B2B energy sector lacks creative/brand DNA—more corporate marketing and stakeholder relations than creative leadership.

title: Fractional Chief Marketing Officer (Startup NWA)
description: About Startup NWA Where a founder sits still shapes whether they get funded. Capital in venture moves through warm introductions and pattern recognition, so a founder’s odds depend as much on who they already know as on what they have built. For founders in the middle of the country, that network gap is the binding constraint. The companies are fundable. They are just harder to find from a desk in Menlo Park. Startup NWA exists to close that gap. It is a program of the Northwest Arkansas Council, the regional organization founded in 1990 by Sam Walton, J.B. Hunt, Don Tyson, and other business leaders to take on the region's hardest problems together. For thirty-five years the Council has backed the work behind the region's growth, across jobs, talent, infrastructure, and quality of life. Startup NWA applies that same instinct to founders, and works across a few fronts: Onward FX (about 85% of this role): our statewide founder-funder exchange, built with the Arkansas Economic Development Commission. Twice a year we bring national investors to Bentonville and sit them across from founders for curated, pre-briefed one-on-one meetings. Every meeting is a focused, one-on-one conversation between a decision-maker and a team that fits their thesis. Ecosystem storytelling: profiling and elevating the founders building here, so the people who should know about them do. The work is already real. A lot of the job is making sure it travels. The Role Reporting to the Executive Director, the Fractional Chief Marketing Officer will own marketing and brand for Startup NWA, with one primary mandate: recruit the right founders, nationally, to Onward FX, and move them through a funnel whose ultimate output is term sheets. This is a hands-on, senior engagement spanning brand strategy, full-funnel demand generation, creative direction, and performance marketing, with real budget and the autonomy to set the plan. This is a fractional, contract engagement based in Northwest Arkansas, with travel to Bentonville and Little Rock around key program moments. We are open to a remote-first arrangement for the right person. The Track Record We are five cycles in. The metrics we hold ourselves to are the ones downstream of the meetings: capital raised, term sheets signed, and companies that kept building. $118M: raised by 90 companies that came through the program. 1 in 4: founders from past cycles signed a term sheet within six months of attending. 990: curated, pre-briefed one-on-one meetings between founders and investors across five events. 330: startups selected from thousands of applicants and matched to investors who lead at their stage. 200+: VC firms in the network, from seed funds writing first checks to multistage platforms. 3x: the venture deal volume in Arkansas since the program began. Key Responsibilities Founder Recruitment: Own the national engine that brings founders to Onward FX: creative strategy, integrated campaigns, and a content-first program that drives demand, conversion, and retention across cycles that now run close to year-round. Recruitment is mandate-driven: you source against the specific theses of the participating investors and corporate strategic partners. Full-Funnel Ownership: Own the funnel and the data behind it, from cold awareness through application, acceptance, and re-engagement. Optimize for qualified applications, measured against what they produce downstream. The top of this funnel is judged at the bottom of it, in term sheets. Omnichannel Marketing: Direct execution across email, paid social, paid search, SEO and GEO, organic social, and earned media, with a clear point of view on the right channel mix for each phase of a cycle. Brand & Messaging: Harden a brand that is already developed: customer profiles, brand pillars, look and feel, and a messaging system that holds up across a primary audience of founders and investors. Channel Partnerships: Build the partner motion (entrepreneur support organizations, VC p
score_reason: Fractional CMO at nonprofit/economic development org lacks creative leadership scope, brand/campaign focus, and no mention of remote work or compensation details.

title: CMO Media/News/Web3 (Equity)
description: Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) - Press Labs Inc. Compensation: Equity until Fundraising with salary range of $140,000 - $200,000 annual. Expected Fundraise Date(s) : We are aiming for a successful pre-seed raise within the next 90 days. title: Creative Strategist / Head of Content
description: Read this before you apply. Most content jobs are scheduling jobs. Someone hands you a calendar, you fill the boxes, nobody measures anything, and the company wonders why content doesn't produce revenue. This is not that job. The AI Solution is an AI infrastructure company. We install AI-led operating systems into businesses for entrepreneurs and real estate professionals. We don't sell courses. We don't sell inspiration. We build systems that clients own. Our founder, Carrie Soave, is the voice of the brand: featured speaker with the Success Magazine Speakers Bureau, eXp Realty AI Alliance leader, and the subject of Artificially Real , a reality show documenting the build of an AI-first company. The brand has one voice. Yours will be invisible. If that bothers you, stop reading. The role You own the creative engine. Idea to analysis. Carrie approves. You execute everything else. You own: Ideation. Content angles, hooks, ad concepts, and campaign themes for two audiences: entrepreneurs and real estate agents. Copy. Scripts, captions, and ad copy written inside a locked, documented voice architecture. You will study it, absorb it, and write in it. Not near it. In it. Creative direction. You write briefs so clear that our production team (editor and videographer) executes without a single clarifying question. Ad creative strategy. Concepts, hooks, and scripts for Meta. You are the input that decides whether paid traffic converts. Media buying belongs to our CMO. Editing belongs to production. The thinking belongs to you. Distribution. Platform strategy, cadence, and repurposing. What goes where, in what format, and why. Analysis. Weekly creative performance reporting. Which hooks won. Which concepts died. What gets iterated. Winners feed back into the system. Losers get killed without sentiment. You report directly to the CEO. You collaborate with the CMO on performance data. You direct a production team that's already built. Who wins this role 2+ years as a creative strategist at a performance creative or DTC agency, or you ran the content and creative engine in-house for a founder-led brand doing $2M+. You can show work with numbers attached. Hook rates. Thumbstop. Booked calls. Revenue influenced. Pretty portfolios without performance data will not get a reply. There are strong ideas across the company, in the product, in marketing, in how they talk about what they’re doing, but it’s all a bit disconnected. Nothing’s broken, it just hasn’t been pulled into a clear, cohesive point of view. They need someone to step in and actually define that. To figure out what this company stands for, what makes it different, and how that should come across in everything they put out into the world. This role is about doing that work properly. Not from a distance, but in it day-to-day. You’d be working closely with the founder and a small, experienced team, taking rough ideas and shaping them into something sharper and more intentional. Some days that’s stepping back and thinking about positioning and narrative, other days you’re deep in the detail, refining copy, pushing creative, or making calls on what feels on-brand and what doesn’t. It needs someone who’s comfortable moving between those levels without overthinking it. They’re looking for someone who’s been through this stage before, where things are still moving quickly and not everything is defined yet. Ideally you’ve helped shape a consumer brand in that kind of environment. You’ll need strong instincts, a good sense of culture, and the confidence to have a clear point of view and stick to it when it matters. The real appeal here is the level of influence. You’re not maintaining an existing brand system, you’re building it from the ground up while the company is still finding its shape. If you like working closely with founders, figuring things out in real time, and having a genuine hand in how something new is understood and talked about, this is the kind of role that doesn’t come up often. title: Creative Director

title: Head of Creative Operations

This is an opportunity to make a significant impact by sh

title: Associate Director, Art Direction
description: WHY DEPT®? We are a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, we create what is next by pioneering ideas, acting fast, and moving further because standing still just is not in our DNA. We are drawn to people who stay curious, move with intent, and never stop inventing. Our culture runs on three values: better together, relentlessly curious, and get sh*t done. It is how we work, how we grow, and how we make things that matter. At DEPT®, you will find the freedom to explore, the space to collaborate, and the trust to make a real impact for our clients, for each other, and for the world we are helping to build. THE ROLE: The Associate Director, Creative is a visual visionary and a tactical leader. You are responsible for defining the aesthetic direction of our key accounts and ensuring that every touchpoint, from social content to large-scale integrated campaigns, is visually stunning and strategically sound. In this role, you bridge the gap between "the big idea" and "the beautiful execution." You are a master of craft who can still design or art direct high-stakes work, but you are equally focused on mentoring a team of designers and art directors to elevate their visual output. WHAT YOU’LL DO: Lead the visual evolution of brands. You’ll oversee the art direction for pitches and flagship projects, ensuring all work meets a world-class standard of design, typography, and composition. Guide a team of junior to mid-level Art Directors and Designers. You’ll provide specific, craft-based feedback that pushes their work further while helping them develop their own visual voices. Take ownership of the visual narrative across multi-channel campaigns. You’ll lead the aesthetic side of "the big idea," ensuring a cohesive look and feel across film, digital, social, and physical experiences. Lead the visual side of production, from selecting photographers and directors to overseeing color grading, retouching, and motion design, ensuring the final product matches the original creative vision. Act as the visual authority in client meetings. You’ll present design systems and art direction with passion and clarity, helping clients understand the strategic "why" behind visual choices. Partner with Creative leadership to evolve design workflows, establish quality standards, and identify new tools or AI-driven technologies to keep the team’s craft future-ready. Foster an inclusive, design-forward culture that encourages experimentation, shared learning, and a relentless pursuit of visual excellence. WHAT YOU BRING: 5+ years of experience in art direction or design within a creative agency or high-growth brand environment or related field. Showcasing a mastery of art direction, layout, typography, and integrated visual storytelling across various platforms. Experience leading small teams or overseeing the work of freelancers and junior creatives. Expert-level knowledge of Figma, Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, etc.), motion design, or AI image generation. The ability to ensure that every visual choice, from color palette to casting, serves the client’s business goals and brand identity. The ability to articulate complex visual concepts to non-visual stakeholders and clients. WHAT DO WE OFFER? We are a collaborative, open-door, best-idea-wins environment that fosters personal and company growth and has fun doing it. Here is a snapshot of our benefits package: Healthcare, Dental, and Vision coverage 401k plan, plus matching PTO Paid Company Holidays Parental Leave WE SUPPORT YOU BEING YOU: DEPT® is an equal opportunity employer (EOE). We believe our work is its best when our people feel safe and free to be themselves. We’re committed to an inclusive and barrier-free recruitment and selection process and workplace, regardless of anyone’s identity, background, beliefs, lifestyle or disabilities. So tell us about any accommodat

title: Head of Games
description: ABOUT WARREN JAMES Warren James is the leading creator commerce partner for top digital talent. We build premium consumer products and brands at the intersection of content, fandom, and commerce - spanning DTC, retail, licensing, and new category expansion. Our work blends creative instinct, operator discipline, and speed. Warren James designs and publishes board games and tabletop products built on creator IP, most recently collaborating with Dropout to create Game Changer: Home Edition (~$6M raised as of early June). This is one of our fastest-growing lines, and we are building it from a hit-driven pipeline into a repeatable, multi-title studio. We are looking for a General Manager to lead WJ Games. The Role The Head of Games owns Warren James Games end to end - the division P&L, the release slate, and the operating system that turns creator IP into a repeatable engine. This is an operator's role with creative taste. Your job is to make the eighth and twelfth title land as reliably as the first. This is a build-and-run leadership role. You will set the slate and the greenlight discipline, decide what we own versus rent, hire and lead the core team, and be a credible partner to creators and their representatives. You can zoom out to run the division like a business and zoom in to pressure-test a launch plan, distribution, a price tier, or a unit-economics model. WHAT YOU'LL OWN Division P&L & Commercial The full Games P&L - revenue, contribution margin by title and channel, and the working capital that defines games/tabletop economics (MOQ, tooling, fulfillment timing) - in partnership with Finance The royalty stack across creator, campaign manager, distributor, and Warren James The greenlight/kill discipline - the campaign average order value architecture and the contribution floor that decide which titles earn effort Crowdfunding End-to-end campaign management across Kickstarter, Gamefound, and emerging platforms - pre-launch audience build, the live campaign, pledge-manager conversion, and the handoff to fulfillment Campaign pricing and tier architecture - the base / hero / collector structure, add-ons, and stretch goals that lift average order value to the contribution floor without breaking audience-product fit Performance marketing - directing the agency layer (e.g., Jellop, Crowdfunding Nerds, BackerKit Services) against funnel economics, with the discipline to scale what's working and kill what isn't, fast Metagame and community engagement - deploying interactive campaign mechanics where the IP warrants it (and recognizing when it doesn't), to drive comments, findability, and sustained funding rather than the typical front-loaded curve Platform strategy - the per-title call on Kickstarter vs. Gamefound vs. direct-to-consumer, and the broader go-to-market sequencing that turns a creator audience into a funded launch Slate Strategy & Sequencing The slate: which titles, formats, at what price tiers, on what cadence (targeting 5–8 releases a year) The launch calendar - staggering releases to avoid concurrency crunches across design, production, and fulfillment The IP filter - a repeatable method for judging which creator / talent IP works as games/tabletop - and the per-title call on crowdfunding vs. retail vs. both, including knowing when not to force a title against its audience The Operating System - the durable asset The playbooks, partner network, and performance data, documented so the studio’s know-how lives in the system, not in any one person’s head The campaign and production playbook, internalized over time so the studio depends less on outside consultants Team & Org Hiring and leading the core team - designer, developer, campaign manager, and production management - and maturing retail into an owned function The freelance designer roster and the external partner layer (crowdfunding agencies, manufacturers, fulfillment, pledge managers) The talent flywheel that lets the studio scale title count without scaling
score_reason: Senior creative leadership in entertainment/gaming with strong operator scope, but P&L-heavy GM role tilts toward general management over creative direction; no remote/location/comp details provided to assess fit.

title: VP, Marketing
description: At Swyfft, we're reshaping the way home insurance and commercial package products are priced and bound. We've created an insurance experience that's smart, instant, and designed to deliver unparalleled customer service. Our focus on lightning-fast quotes and seamless claims servicing is powered by cutting-edge technology and an Agent and Customer-centric approach that sets us apart in the industry. Joining Swyfft means becoming part of a dynamic team of forward-thinkers who thrive on moving fast and delivering exceptional products. We pride ourselves on fostering an environment where creativity and positive energy thrive. As we continue to grow and expand, we're on the lookout for experienced professionals like you to join us in transforming the insurance landscape. If you're passionate about leveraging technology to provide the best customer service experience and are ready to be a part of our journey, we welcome you to explore opportunities at Swyfft! About the Position: We are searching for an experienced VP of Marketing with P&C insurance experience to drive the strategic direction of how we present ourselves to agents, brokers, policyholders, carrier partners, and the marketplace. This is a fast-paced, roll up your sleeves position that partners closely with our sales, underwriting, and executive leadership teams to amplify our brand and grow our distribution network. We need fresh ideas, great leadership and a communication plan that articulates our value proposition in highly competitive and often volatile markets. We're not looking for a traditional marketing executive who manages agencies and approves campaigns. We're looking for a builder — someone who is energized by the challenge of establishing a brand in difficult markets, who understands the nuances of insurance distribution, and who can move fast without sacrificing quality. Swyfft is about to embark on a very exciting time filled with growth and opportunity. If this gets you excited, then we need to talk! Previous experience leading a small marketing team with varying levels of experience is required. This position reports into our SVP, Sales & Marketing. *This position is a 100% remote U.S. based opportunity that can be based in one of the following states only: AL, AZ, FL, GA, KY, LA, MA, MO, NC, NJ, NY, OH, OR, PA, SC, TX, UT, VA, WA, WI. Some travel for day-to-day work, team meetings, and training will be required. Key Responsibilities: (What you'll be asked to do) Brand Strategy & Positioning Define and champion Swyfft’s brand identity across all markets, ensuring consistency and relevance with agents, carriers, and policyholders. Develop messaging frameworks that clearly communicate our expertise in CAT-exposed coastal regions and California markets. Differentiate our brand in competitive markets where trust, capacity, and responsiveness are key. Distribution & Channel Marketing Design and execute agent and broker recruitment campaigns to rapidly grow our distribution network across our current states and into new markets as we expand. Build targeted outreach strategies for coastal property specialists, surplus lines producers, and independent agents operating in admitted and non-admitted markets. Develop co-marketing programs and collateral that support our distribution partners in the field. Digital Presence & Content Oversee digital marketing channels including website, email, SEO/SEM, LinkedIn, and emerging platforms relevant to the insurance distribution space. Create compelling content, thought leadership articles, market updates, CAT event communications, and producer resources that build credibility and drive engagement. Leverage data analytics to measure campaign performance and optimize spend across traditional and social media channels. Market Intelligence & Product Storytelling Monitor and synthesize industry trends, regulatory developments, and competitive dynamics across all active states with a particular focus on Florida, Texas, Louisiana

title: Director of Brand & Content
description: I'm Michael, founder and CEO of Product.ai. I've been building commerce technology for 16 years — profitable, bootstrapped, 100% owner. No board. No outside investors. No one telling us what to ship. We're building the truth engine for shopping. Every AI tool summarizes polluted data and calls it an answer. We verify. I'm hiring a Director of Brand & Content — a peer, not an apprentice. Someone who arrived having already owned brand at a real company, who brings opinionated craft into the room, and who has explicit permission to push back on me. The brand we have to build is bigger than I can hold alone. I need a co-author, not a ghostwriter. Why I Need This Person Product.ai is at the inflection where the mission needs a brand architecture that survives beyond me. Right now the brand IS me — my voice, my LinkedIn, my publishing rhythm. That doesn't scale to where we're going. I need someone who has done this before. Built a brand function from scratch, or owned one through a major reposition. Someone who brings their own taste, their own track record of saying no to the CEO and being right. Not a voice competitor — I'll still write the canonical mid-form pieces. But the brand system, the institutional voice, the recruiting brand, the dual-brand architecture between Product.ai and SimplyCodes — those need a co-architect with peer authority. If you're the operator who owned brand at a fast-growing company through a category-creation moment, who's tired of being the senior IC who can't get the decisions made, who wants direct CEO access and the authority to build the brand the way it should be built — that's who I'm looking for. The System You'll Need to Model A dual-brand architecture. Product.ai (truth engine, intelligence-led) and SimplyCodes (coupon engine, revenue base). Different audiences, shared intelligence, one company. The decision tree for any co-brand call lives in your head, then on the wall. A founder voice that has to remain mine. I write the canonical thinking. You build the institutional voice that lives around it — the company-page voice, the recruiting voice, the partnership voice. You define the boundary cleanly so neither voice cannibalizes the other. An AI-era citation landscape. 85% of the citations that matter now happen off our domain — Reddit, Wikipedia, YouTube, GitHub, G2, Trustpilot. AEO is mostly a mislabeled PR + community function. You bring the strategic frame for how a brand earns citations in this landscape, then orchestrate the surface coverage. A recruiting brand that has to magnetize 10x operators. We've built the substrate — the Frontier Operator Codex, 49 trial projects, six discipline pages at product.ai/join/. You own the editorial layer: JD voice, candidate-page narrative, the magnet content stream that pulls the operators we can't find through job boards. A 16-person company that has to execute like 160. The brand has to do load-bearing work for talent, partnerships, press, customers, and AI agents simultaneously. Centralization is the rule; sprawl is the failure mode. If reading that list energizes you, keep reading. If it feels overwhelming or underspecified, this isn't the right fit. What You Will Own Brand architecture. The brand operating system that the next 30 hires inherit. Verbal identity, narrative architecture, the dual-brand decision tree. Sole authority — you don't reconcile across three other VPs, you decide. The institutional voice. Product.ai company-page, SimplyCodes company-page, the recruiting-brand voice, the partnership voice. Distinct from my personal voice and explicitly designed not to compete with it. You build the system that lets us speak as a company without diluting how we speak as me. Editorial direction. Final say on what we publish, where, when, in what voice. Pushback authority on me when my draft is wrong for the surface. This is the partnership posture — not apprentice, not deferential. The recruiting magnet. The Frontier Operator Codex, the 49 trial
score_reason: Senior brand leadership at inflection point with real creative scope, but no remote work confirmation, no salary disclosed, and bootstrapped startup carries execution risk vs. Travis's corporate/agency background.

title: Head of Content Marketing
description: About Us We are Playbook, a leading creator platform for fitness, health, and wellness. Our mission is to help fitness creators build sustainable businesses, while enabling hundreds of thousands of users to live healthier lives. We are a fast-growing company in the creator economy space, operating as a remote-first, product-driven team that values ownership, direct communication, and a strong growth mindset. We believe in "drivers, not passengers": everyone is encouraged to take responsibility, think proactively, and act like an owner. Role Overview We are hiring a Head of Content Marketing to own and grow Playbook's content function. This is a senior, high-ownership role for an operator who has built content engines before and wants to do it again, with a sharper brand and a bigger upside this time. You will set the strategy, build the team, and ship the work. The first phase is hands-on; we want a leader who can produce at a high level themselves while the function is being built, and who knows how to balance short term priorities with the long term bets. This role works closely with leadership across Growth, Product Marketing, and the Creator Team, and reports to the Head of Growth . We are not looking for a prescriptive role. We are looking for the person who develops the playbook. Responsibilities Own the content strategy across organic search, social, video, written, podcast, and creator enablement. Define what we do, what we do not do, and why. Set and maintain the editorial bar. Establish the production workflows, the quality standard, and the cadence the team can sustain. Lead with the work. In your first phase, you will write, produce, and ship a meaningful share of the content yourself. We expect senior leaders to model the bar. Build the team. Identify the right roles, hire the right people, and develop them. Drive measurable outcomes. Define a small set of content KPIs and build the reporting that ties content output to pipeline, signups, and revenue. Partner across the company. Coordinate with Growth, Product Marketing, Sales, and Product. Your work should compound theirs and vice versa. Use modern tools to expand output. Identify and deploy the AI workflows, automations, and editorial systems that let a small content team punch above its weight. Requirements 8+ years in content marketing or content-led growth. You can show traffic, ranked positions, leads, and revenue impact tied to your work. Hands-on producer. You can write the article, script the video, brief the editor, and approve the final cut. You are senior, but you are not above the work. Cross-format fluency. SEO articles, video scripts, social, case studies, podcasts, long-form. You do not need to be elite at every format, but you need to be strong across the board. Deep working knowledge of how SaaS and creator businesses actually grow: positioning, offers, lead generation, conversion, retention. You can hold your own in a tactical conversation with a sophisticated operator. Strong organic search foundation. Keyword research, technical hygiene, internal linking, content briefs, and a credible point of view on AI search. Track record of hiring and developing content talent. You know how to interview, coach, and unblock. Operator mindset. You push for the best hook, not the "good enough" hook. You measure what you ship. You do not settle. Exceptional communicator. We are remote-first and async-friendly; your voice has to carry the weight. Nice to Have Lived experience in fitness, health, wellness, or an adjacent creator vertical. Familiarity with the creator economy ecosystem and the platforms creators use. Background as a creator yourself. You have built and monetized your own audience. Experience operating a podcast or video channel that found an audience. Comfort working with data tools, BI dashboards, and modern SEO platforms. A network you can tap for collaborations, and referrals. What we offer Your work will directly affect creators and users on
score_reason: Senior remote content leadership with strategic scope, but DTC/SaaS focus and growth-marketing DNA differ from Travis's integrated advertising and brand-building background.

title: Head of Content Marketing
description: At Skan AI, you'll be part of the team pioneering the context engine for human and agentic execution, bringing context from enterprise operators, systems, and processes to power how the world's largest organizations execute their most complex, mission-critical work. WHY JOIN SKAN AI We're in hyper-growth mode at exactly the right moment in history. As enterprises race to adopt agentic AI, we're uniquely positioned to deliver the clear signal they desperately need: a platform that trains and grounds AI Agents in trillions of real execution signals, enabling reliable, compliant automation of their most complex processes. Backed by Dell Technologies Capital and other leading investors, we're the only company that can bridge the gap between AI's promise and enterprise reality, making us perfectly positioned to define the agentic era for modern enterprises. Join our diverse, collaborative team of 200+ innovators solving category-defining challenges at the intersection of AI, process intelligence, and enterprise work. Here, diverse perspectives fuel breakthrough thinking, cross-functional collaboration is the norm, and your contributions will directly transform how Fortune 500 companies operate. This is your opportunity to shape the future of work itself alongside talented colleagues who challenge, support, and inspire you every day. ABOUT THE ROLE We're looking for a content leader who builds and runs the engine, not just fills it. You'll own Skan's editorial voice, thought leadership program, and content function end-to-end, translating complex technology into narratives that move C-suite buyers at Fortune 500 companies. This isn't about safe, forgettable enterprise copy. It's about defining how a category talks about itself. You'll set the strategy, lead the team, and roll up your sleeves when it matters, spanning thought leadership, demand generation, product marketing, social media, SEO/AEO, customer marketing, sales enablement, and executive content. title: Founding Content & Creative Lead
description: Escargot · NYC · full-time · founding team · $100,000 - $150,000 + meaningful equity and benefits You make the content. You own the craft. About Escargot Escargot started with greeting cards — but cards are the wedge. We're building a new kind of digital-to-physical social network: one that helps people keep track of the relationships that matter, act on the moments they usually miss, and turn those moments into physical things — cards, gifts, keepsakes, the little objects that prove a life happened. Social media became performance. Texting became the default for everything. But the things people actually save — a card on the fridge, a note in a drawer, a ticket stub — still carry disproportionate meaning. That's the space we're building in: the bridge between digital convenience and physical meaning. We've raised $3M from South Park Commons and Hannah Grey, 100,000+ people use Escargot, we have a 4.6 App Store rating, and people are already framing our cards on their walls. We're a small NYC team founded by Aaron Albert (CEO) and Andrew Gold (CTO). Now we need to make Escargot feel much bigger in the world. Why this role exists We need one person who is the creative brain behind everything Escargot makes — and good enough with their own hands to make it. Not a content calendar. Not a brand director who lives in decks. The person who can look at any channel, invent the creative idea for it, and then art-direct, shoot, edit, and design it into something people actually want to watch. What you own • The creative concept for every channel — the idea of what we make, before anyone picks up a camera • Art direction, photography, video, editing, and design — the craft that brings it to life • Short-form video end to end: hook, script, shoot, cut • The repeatable formats that make Escargot recognizable • The brief and creative direction for any creators we work with • The quality bar — if something's dead on arrival, you kill it before it ships The kind of work you'll make One week it's a format you concept, shoot on a phone, and cut three ways by tomorrow. The next it's the creative idea for a popup the Growth Lead is running, a campaign that needs to actually look like something, or directing a creator so the thing they make doesn't feel like an ad. You'll work closely with Aaron on taste and voice early on. You make a lot, you move fast, and you care whether the work is good — not whether it looks expensive. Where the line is We're hiring two founding roles that build one machine. This is the Creative Lead. Here's the clean line between the two: • Owns — Creative Lead owns what people see; Organic Growth Lead owns how we grow. • Works in — Creative: ideas and craft (concept, art direction, photo, video, edit, design). Growth: plays (channels, hacks, events, popups, stunts, partnerships). • Creators — Creative briefs and directs what they make; Growth sources, closes, and runs the relationship. • A stunt or event — Creative dreams up what it actually is and makes it land; Growth decides if it's worth running and makes it happen. Both of you invent, both of you execute, and both of you answer the same question: did this make someone actually want to send something? You're probably a fit if • You're the idea person and the maker — you can concept it, shoot it, edit it, and design around it yourself • You have taste, and the body of work to prove it (show us) • You get platform-native short-form video, down to why the first frame decides everything • You'd rather make ten things and learn than polish one • You can feel when something is generic, cringe, or over-polished — and fix it You're probably not a fit if • You need a full brief and a crew before you can make anything • Your reel is gorgeous brand films that very few people watched • Most of your output is decks about the work, not the work • A blank page with no playbook worries you more than it excites you Why this matters This is bigger than making content for a card company.
score_reason: Founding creative leadership at a compelling product company, but NYC-based (not remote), early-stage salary ($100-150K) below Travis's seniority level, and lacks the advertising/brand campaign scale he's built.

title: Senior, Art Director

It is how we work, how we grow, and how we make things that matter. At DEPT®, you will find the freedom to explore, the space to collaborate, and the trust to make a real impact for our clients, for each other, and for the world we are helping to build. Someone who can translate strategy into smart, engaging creative that hooks niche audiences. You’ll partner closely with a team of Copywriters, Video Editors, Content Creators, Visual Designers and report into the Director, Creative, collaborating to bring creative briefs to life across digital, social, and video channels. You’ll help shape always-on social and campaign direction, lead visual execution, and ensure design excellence from kickoff through delivery. You’ll play a crucial role in owning your workstream and consistently raising the creative bar This role is perfect for someone who thrives in creative collaboration, is autonomous in ideation, enjoys rolling up their sleeves to execute, and has a strong point of view on what makes effective content in the digital world. Own the work from end to end. Concepting and producing polished, on-brand, performance-minded assets that meet timelines and briefs. As a Principal, Art Director, you will lead by doing, driving execution while strategically delegating portions of our projects to our junior team members to support efficiency, mentorship, and delivery while remaining accountable for the final output. Navigate ambiguity with CD support, adapt to shifting priorities, and bring proactive solutions to creative and production challenges. These solutions should come in the form of daily brainstorming and inspiration sharing with the team. Approach every situation with curiosity, kindness, and accountability to help foster an inclusive and collaborative team environment. WHAT YOU BRING: 2+ years of experience in visual design with a strong portfolio showcasing digital campaigns, social-first content, and video-driven work. Prior social agency experience or relevant experience with a track record of navigating high-volume, high-visibility accounts. On-set and content production experience (domestic or international), including working with video directors, editors, and producers. Strong client presentation and communication skills - you can confidently present ideas, take feedback in stride, and advocate for great creative. Excellent design sensibility and a strategic eye for layout, typography, motion, and branding. Additionally, deep knowledge of social best practices for optimal performance. A proactive, collaborative mindset - you bring energy to brainstorms, jump in where needed, and support your teammates with generosity and respect. A creative portfolio that shows your eye for design, your

title: Design Director (Pepsi)

Innovation & Creativity: Develop wildly innovative and culturally relevant concepts that resonate with target consumer. Maintain a high level of collaboration to ensure cohesive brand narratives. Budget & Resource Management: Manage assigned project budgets, ensuring creative solutions are developed within financial constraints. Collaborate with internal teams to optimize resources and achieve project goals. Performance Metrics: Track and measure the success of creative initiatives through key performance indicators (KPIs) such as PR impact, consumer engagement, earned media, and sales performance. Location, confirmed job-related skills, experience, and education will be considered in setting actual starting salary.
