Build Figma plugins or design-system integrations when the customer's creative team works there. • Build thin front-ends (or conversational experiences) and light back-ends (App Builder, Azure Functions, AWS Lambda). Ship a working URL the customer uses this quarter, not a PowerPoint. • Build custom workflow nodes when existing ones fall short. Push reusable ones upstream. • Integrate with the customer's stack — AEM Assets, Workfront, Frame.io, their DAM, their PIM, their brand portal. Negotiate auth, debug API quirks, own the edge cases. • Train the customer's team. Document. Hand off cleanly so the customer self-serves when you rotate to the next engagement. • Demo your work yourself — script, produce, edit. Show real creative outcomes, not abstract slides. Experience • 5–10 years straddling creative production AND technical building. You have shipped both: real creative work (motion, design, video, 3D, photography, brand systems) AND real technical work (apps, integrations, plugins, automation). • Creative content supply chain fluency. You understand how briefs become assets, how brand systems and design tokens work, how master files become channel variants, how creative teams hand off through Figma / PSD / INDD to DAM and review-and-approval. • Firefly Services + Workflow Builder fluency — or evidence you ramp on a new API surface in days, not months. Adobe ecosystem depth (AEM, Workfront, Frame.io, App Builder) is a strong plus, as is Figma plugin / API fluency. • Vibe-coding with judgment. You use Cursor / Claude Code / Copilot daily and ship fast. You also know when AI output is wrong, when error handling is missing, when the prompt needs more context. Production code, not prototype-only. • Extreme ownership and a GSD attitude. When the integration breaks before a CMO demo, you fix it — you don't escalate. You own outcomes end-to-end, from broken auth to a missed brand-compliance check. You ship ugly first and refuse to let process slow you down. • Extreme customer orientation. You hold the room with CMOs, creative directors, and heads of IT. You hear a customer pain and respond with a credible, impact-focused, creative solution. You translate tech to ROI without losing the engineers in the room. • A great nose for the money. You instinctively connect what you build to business outcomes (ie: time-to-activation, cost per variant, brand-compliance pass rate, campaign velocity.) • A passion for coding and tinkering. A deep interest in generative AI and creative workflows. You explore adjacent orchestration tools — ComfyUI for visual workflow prototyping, n8n / Zapier / Power Automate for low-code connectors, LangGraph / LangChain for agentic flows. • A public portfolio — link or PDF at application. Must show shipped creative work AND shipped technical artifacts. This is a hard requirement; applications without a portfolio will not be reviewed. What we're not looking for: • Backend-only or distributed-systems engineers with no creative practitioner background. • Pure designers or creatives who can't stand up a working integration. • ML / AI researchers who are not interested in customer outcomes. • PMs / consultants who don't ship. Every team member writes code AND produces creative work.

Today, that space is a conference center, and that spirit of design-forward thinking in banking feels almost extinct. Mercury believes that level of care and craft in design has been lost in modern finance and we’re building a brand that brings it back. We're a financial technology company building products that feel extraordinary to use. title: Principal AI Creative Director, POD
description: Description Advertising is being remade. The brands that win the next decade will be the ones that figure out how to deliver creative that is personal, performant, and produced at a scale no human team can match alone. Generative AI is the unlock. But generation alone is not the advantage. The advantage belongs to the system that connects creation to delivery to learning, and gets better with every impression. Amazon Ads is building that system. We are investing in the models, the infrastructure, and the product intelligence to make personalized, brand-aligned creative a default capability for every advertiser, not a luxury reserved for the largest budgets. The work is early, the stakes are high, and the opportunity to define how this category develops is real. We are building that system. We need a Principal AI Creative Director to define what great looks like inside it and to make that definition teachable, scalable, and machine-readable. Key job responsibilities As Principal AI Creative Director for Personalization Optimization and Design (POD), you are the human judgment layer at the center of Amazon's AI creative system. Generative AI has commoditized asset creation. The durable differentiator is now creative direction: the taste, the standards, the brand intelligence, and the systematic thinking that ensures our system produces creative that builds brands, not just optimizes clicks. This is not a role about using AI tools, though you must be among the most fluent practitioners working today. It is about understanding what makes a great commercial, a great piece of content, a great brand moment, and then decomposing that understanding into primitives: the story arc, the color logic, the compositional tension, the emotional beat, the brand signal. Those primitives are what our systems learn from, replicate, and eventually govern at scale. You will operate as a principal-level individual contributor with org-wide influence, partnering directly with design, engineering, science, and product leadership across every surface (POD) owns. A day in the life Creative Direction And Quality Standards Define and own the creative quality bar for AI-generated assets across POD's full portfolio, including images, video, copy, and multi-format compositions. Establish brand intelligence frameworks that ensure AI outputs are aesthetically coherent, emotionally resonant, and brand-safe at scale. Act as the senior creative editor for AI-generated content, distinguishing exceptional work from "good enough" outputs and articulating the difference in decomposable, teachable terms. Decomposing Creative into Primitives. Lead the Systemization of Taste: Break down what makes great creative into its constituent elements: narrative structure, color theory, compositional hierarchy, emotional arc, pacing, and brand signal. Translate these primitives into structured frameworks, including prompt libraries, creative briefs, quality rubrics, and annotation schemas, that inform model training, DCO reward signals, and creative tooling. Define the primitives that power aesthetic moderation: the signals and thresholds that allow our systems to evaluate, filter, and rank AI-generated content without requiring human review of every asset. Partner with applied science and engineering to operationalize these as machine-readable inputs. Hands-On Production And Prototyping Produce high-quality creative content independently across modalities, including image, video, and copy, using the leading AI creation tools. Bring that same hands-on fluency to prototyping: build working creative prototypes that demonstrate new customer experiences, surface trade-offs, and create decision points for design, product, and engineering leadership. This role operates in a demo-driven culture where prototypes move decisions. You are expected to contribute directly to that culture, not just direct others within it. Maintain current, deep fluency across tools including text-to-image (Mid

We’re backed by CRV, Susa Ventures, Fifth Wall, and leaders from Plaid, Segment, ServiceTitan, and Procore. title: Director of Communications
description: A Tolan is a friend you shape, and who shapes you in turn. A personal AI you name and make your own, who remembers what matters to you and gently moves you from reflection to action. A friend who points you back toward your real life, not deeper into a screen. Over the last year, Tolan has quietly built a growing, deeply committed community of more than 100,000 women, who are making Tolan part of their lives as they juggle work, parenting, and busy households. We're hiring a Communications Director to tell the story of the people who've found a real friend in their Tolan. The role Tolan is a new product. It's also the leading edge of the most important social shift of the next fifty years: people working out their relationship with AI, and what that does to their relationships with each other. Your job is to tell two stories. One is Tolan itself, the product and the difference it makes in the lives of the people it serves. The other is bigger, about Tolan the company and where it sits in this larger rethinking of how we relate to AI, and to each other. You'll tell both through an optimistic lens, grounded in evidence. We take a research-driven approach, led by Lily Doyle, and our studies show Tolan measurably reducing users' anxiety and improving their wellbeing. This role requires balancing prestige narrative-setting with hands-on execution. You'll pitch and partner with existing publications, and build the channels and assets we own ourselves. What you'll do Own the narrative Work directly with the CEO and founders to define what Tolan is, what it isn't, and why it matters. Write the messaging that works for Call Her Daddy and The Atlantic in the same week. Keep our users at the center of the narrative. Drive earned media Build relationships with journalists, editors, and producers across lifestyle, culture, tech, mental health, and entertainment, landing everything from a New York Times feature to a Drew Barrymore segment. Land stories about Tolan and the broader shift in how people relate to AI, making the Tolan Research Team a voice the press trusts on where AI helps and where it hurts. Reach the heartland, not just NY and LA: regional TV, women's magazines, podcast networks, faith-adjacent media. Get close to our community and tell the stories of the lives Tolan has made better. Build our own media Make the media we own: original video, podcasts, and editorial that tell Tolan's story in our voice. Keep a steady drumbeat of original content so Tolan stays in the cultural conversation between press moments. Who you are You've built consumer brands people love, with millennial women at the heart of the audience. You've done it early, at companies without a machine already running, so you know how to make noise without a big budget or a team to lean on. You're senior enough to set the strategy and hands-on enough to write the pitch yourself. You move easily between earned and owned media. You can land a story in a national outlet and build the channel that tells the next one. And you can point to the proof: specific coverage you placed and specific assets you built, work you led, not just contributed to. Bonus points if you have your own platform: a Substack, a podcast, an audience that follows your work. The best person for this has built attention from nothing, because they've done it for themselves. You don't need a traditional comms background. If you're exceptional and you can show it, this role is yours. Compensation and logistics Location: SF preferred. Open to remote, with a preference for LA or NYC. Compensation: competitive salary and meaningful equity, set to experience. Reports to: CEO.

You will also serve as a Secondary Lead, advising and reviewing how each product team applies the IP Bible. Creative oversight of the IP Bible: Serve as the final creative decision maker for all content produced based on the IP Bible Content creation and management at the IP level: Define creative direction and manage quality across narrative, art, music, and cinematics from a holistic IP perspective, rather than on a product basis IP Bible advisory and review: Advise product teams on how to apply the IP Bible and review creative deliverables to ensure alignment with IP standards Creative alignment with EPD (Franchise Group Head): Collaborate with leadership to align creative direction and structure decision making External partner creative guidance: Deliver IP creative guidelines to external developers and partners, and review their deliverables Creative expansion: Explore new creative possibilities for the IP and establish standards for emerging formats such as media mix and brand collaborations Requirements Able to travel internationally without restriction 10+ years of creative leadership experience in game or media IP Ability to define direction and judge quality across the full creative spectrum, including world building, narrative, and visual identity Integrative perspective and communication skills spanning multiple creative domains (art, narrative, music, video, etc) Experience delivering creative guidelines to and reviewing work from external developers or partners Ability to structure and present creative rationale and options to senior decision makers Preferred Qualifications Experience leading creative direction for a global game IP Multilingual communication skills (English, Mandarin, or other languages) Experience building or operating an IP Bible or World Bible Experience expanding IP creatively beyond games into other media (video, publishing, music, etc.) Experience maintaining creative consistency across multiple concurrent projects Experience in creative direction at the franchise or universe level

You’ll help get the right operators, executives and investors in the room - then make Firstwork the company they associate with the future of labor markets. title: VP, Growth Marketing
description: The TL:DR - Who we’re looking for We’re looking for a full-stack senior growth leader who has scaled a CPG / DTC brand to $75M+ in revenue, managed a cross-functional growth team, overseen millions in paid spend, has polished leadership qualities and is a deeply creative strategist. Our ideal candidate has a deep understanding of how to drive growth organically (celebrity founder brand experience huge plus!), build repeatable, scalable systems for UGC, affiliates, and paid creative and has strong understanding of all paid channels. We want someone deeply creative who is constantly brainstorming offers, campaigns, growth hacks and funnels, and then driving the team to enthusiastically launch, learn and iterate. You’re equal parts creative and analytical, you love using AI to work smarter and you deeply understand how to scale subscription revenue to build a CAC-to-LTV money machine. About Lineage Founded by Paul Saladino MD and Anthony Gustin DC, Lineage Provisions exists to make animal-based nutrition easy with a high standard for quality, nutrient-density, taste, and transparency, and to make the world's best products sourced from the world's best producers and farms. 💪 Why Lineage Provisions? It’s time that someone give Big Food a Big Middle Finger. We think that the majority of health products online are just a bunch of garbage with fancy labels trying to sell trendy items for a quick buck. We make the best products we possibly can to help humans eat like humans. We believe that animal-based nutrition is the cornerstone for proper human health. We exist to make adhering to this lifestyle easy and delicious. We also are passionate proponents of regenerative agriculture. Regenerative agriculture is the only way we can produce food indefinitely. The vast majority of how we currently produce food is destroying our ecosystems which we depend on for survival. The more we support regenerative agriculture, the more we support a food system that can continue to feed people in the future. There is no other option. Eating a hyper local and seasonal diet is the best way to approach nutrition, but the reality is most food is purchased in packaged form. We aim to drive a wedge into that industry by sourcing from the best farms, making the best products, and displacing those that cut corners and sell lies. If we’re successful with Lineage Provisions, we will help transition farms from conventional to regenerative agriculture, all while nourishing the people who are supporting us to do so. And we need your help! About the Role We’re hiring a VP of Growth to own the entire growth engine at Lineage. This is not a channel role. This is not a “manage agencies” role. This is a build, lead, and scale the machine role. You will architect and run a modern DTC growth system across: Paid acquisition Organic / founder-led growth Creative strategy Affiliate + partnerships Lifecycle + retention Offer + funnel design You’ll lead a cross-functional team and be directly responsible for driving new customer growth, CAC efficiency, and LTV expansion . What You'll Own Growth Strategy & Playbooks Build and continuously evolve Lineage’s growth playbooks Design scalable systems for: Paid acquisition (Meta-first, multi-channel expansion) UGC + creator pipelines Affiliate / performance partnerships Founder-led organic growth (critical) Translate strategy into clear weekly execution + KPIs Paid Acquisition At-scale Oversee multi-million dollar annual spend Drive performance across: Meta (core) Google / YouTube Emerging channels (TikTok, etc.) Optimize to contribution, not vanity ROAS Continuously push new audience + creative expansion Creative Strategy Leadership Own the creative engine , not just performance Build repeatable systems for: High-volume UGC production Ad concept pipelines (hooks, angles, offers) Iteration velocity Partner tightly with content + founder to unlock scroll-stopping creative Founder-led Growth Flywheel Leverage and scale Dr. Paul Saladino
score_reason: Strong creative + strategic fit for DTC scaling, but growth marketing lane is adjacent to Travis's core CD/brand leadership DNA; no remote confirmation, salary/location/timezone not specified.

title: Sr. Director, Commercialization

From there, we expanded the possibility for sustainable furniture with Sactionals, a customizable modular sectional that can be endlessly rearranged to evolve with you. Our product line has grown a lot since our Sacs days, but our goal will always remain the same – to create truly adaptable, comfortable furniture that can be with you for life. We aim to not only excel within our industry but also make a meaningful impact on the world. Establish and lead a consistent meeting cadence that aligns product, service, technology, marketing, CX, and operations around launch readiness and priorities. Serve as the single point of accountability for go-to-market orchestration across the organization. 18-Month Launch Planning & Road Mapping Develop, maintain, and communicate an integrated 18-month commercialization calendar that spans product, service, and technology launches. Work with internal Brand and Marketing partners to develop and maintain the commercialization calendar to ensure alignment on product launches and go to market initiatives across all channels. Ensure sequencing, dependencies, and capacity considerations are clearly understood and proactively managed. Balance speed to market with operational readiness and customer experience excellence. Launch Execution & Risk Management Act as the executive escalation owner for initiatives with elevated risk, complexity, or cross-functional tension. Drive fast, structured problem-solving to mitigate timeline, cost, or experience impacts. Partner closely with Finance and functional leaders to quantify risk

The function reports to the CMO and partners closely with Technical Product Marketing on product visualization, Demand Generation on campaign creative, PR & Communications on earned content design and executive content, and Web & Digital on the web visual surface. As an AI company, Seekr should be visibly working at the frontier of what AI makes possible in video; the work should make that obvious the moment someone hits play, without ever tipping into generic AI slop. Own the blend of AI-generated and live-action. The strongest work sits at the seam - generative sequences woven with real footage and b-roll, where AI extends what a camera could capture rather than replacing it. Know when a shot should be filmed, when it should be generated, and when the power is in the cut between the two. Direct the external creative agency relationship - scope, standards, budget, deliverables. Where the agency is delivering, scale the engagement. Partner with PR & Communications on executive content design, earned content visualization, and social creative - including the video and motion content that increasingly carries executive thought leadership. Partner with the GTM team on event creative - booth graphics, signage, presentation design, on-stage production for federal and commercial events. Partner with Web & Digital on the web visual standards and the web-as-brand-surface. Build and

title: Head of Retention & Creator Partnerships
description: About The Nordstick The Nordstick makes innovative home fitness equipment, and we're growing triple digits year over year. We're a small, fast-moving team that wins on product and execution, not headcount or process. We've proven the product and the demand — now we're building the retention and creator engine that compounds it. This is a role for someone who wants real ownership and the room to build something from the ground up, reporting directly to the founder with no layers in between. The role in one line Own our owned channels (email + SMS), own affiliate and its profitability, and partner with our team to build a creator flywheel that turns product seeding and affiliate into a compounding, ROI-positive growth machine. We mean flywheel, not funnel. Outreach feeds creative, creative feeds performance data, performance data feeds smarter outreach — every loop should make the next one cheaper and better. If you build it right, it spins faster over time with less push. That's the job. What you'll own Email & SMS, end to end — strategy, calendar, flows, segmentation, list health, and the revenue these channels are accountable for. You set the roadmap and you're on the hook for the number. Affiliate profitability — not just recruiting affiliates and chasing volume, but owning contribution margin after commission and product cost. You'll know which partners actually make us money and which just look busy. The creator flywheel — sourcing and outreach, product seeding, briefing, white-label creative, performance review, and feeding what you learn back into who we work with next. You'll build the loop and keep it spinning with our team. Channel orchestration — making email, SMS, affiliate, and creator content work as one system instead of fighting over the same customer's attention and budget. What success looks like First 30 days: You've audited every flow, the affiliate program, and our current creator efforts, and you've come back with a prioritized list of where money is being left on the table. First 90 days: Owned channels have a clear roadmap and improving revenue, the affiliate program is being measured on real profitability, and the first version of the creator flywheel is live and producing. First year: The flywheel is compounding — creator outreach, seeding, and affiliate are demonstrably more efficient than when you started, and owned channels are a reliable, growing revenue line. You're probably right for this if You've been in the weeds — at an agency, a fast DTC brand, or both — actually building programs, not just managing dashboards or handing off to a team. You think in systems and loops, and you have strong, defensible opinions about what's worth doing and what's a waste of time and budget. You can tell a genuinely profitable creator from one with good-looking numbers, and you understand why most product seeding fails — and how to make it not. You've killed something that was working because something better existed, and you can explain why. You're comfortable owning a number and reporting directly to a founder who'll move fast with you. Tools — experience with a modern ESP/SMS platform (Klaviyo or similar), affiliate/creator platforms, and the analytics to actually measure profitability rather than vanity metrics. Logistics — Fully remote. Competitive base plus performance bonus tied to the growth you drive.
score_reason: Growth-stage DTC role with real ownership but lacks senior creative leadership scope, no remote/location clarity, and missing traditional CD/brand architecture—better suited for a Head of Growth than a Creative Director.

title: VP Product – Ads Experiences
description: What we’re building and why we’re building it. Fetch helps people live rewarded every day, with a vision to become the rewards destination for everyone. We turn everyday activities into meaningful rewards, whether it’s grocery shopping, grabbing a quick meal, or playing a favorite mobile game. To date, we’ve awarded more than $1 billion in Fetch Points to our users. Each day, more than 13 million receipts are submitted on Fetch, providing visibility into over $212 billion in gross merchandise value. This creates the largest retail-agnostic, SKU-level view of household spending, powering Fetch as an outcomes-based advertising platform that helps brands acquire and retain lifelong consumers. The Fetch app is available on the App Store and Google Play, with more than 6 million five-star reviews from a highly engaged and loyal user base. It’s not just our users who believe in Fetch: with investments from Softbank, ICONIQ, DST, Greycroft, and partnerships ranging from challenger brands to Fortune 500 companies, Fetch is reshaping how brands and consumers connect in the marketplace. When you work at Fetch, you play a vital role in a platform that drives brand loyalty and creates lifelong consumers with the power of Fetch points. User and partner success are at the heart of everything we do, and we extend that same commitment to our employees. Flexi

description: About Escargot Escargot started with greeting cards, but cards are the wedge. We’re building a new kind of digital-to-physical social network: one that helps people keep track of the relationships that matter, act on meaningful moments, and turn those moments into physical objects, gifts, cards, memories, and keepsakes. We believe people are craving more real, tangible, emotionally specific ways to connect. Social media became mostly performance and entertainment. Texting became the default for everything. Meanwhile, the things people actually save — cards, photos, notes, ticket stubs, fridge magnets, the weird little objects that prove a life happened — still carry disproportionate meaning. That is the space we’re building in: the bridge between digital convenience and physical meaning. We’ve raised $3M, we’re a small team, and people are already framing Escargot cards on their walls. Now we need someone to help make Escargot feel much bigger in the world. We’re looking for a hands-on creative operator who can figure out how Escargot grows top of funnel through whatever actually works: organic content, creator collaborations, stunts, events, social experiments, partnerships, community ideas, founder-led storytelling, street-level activations, weird internet formats, and campaigns people want to talk about. Your job is to make Escargot culturally impossible to ignore — then turn that attention into real curiosity, traffic, sends, and momentum. You’ll have a huge amount of creative freedom and autonomy. Probably more than you’ve had in any previous job. But autonomy here means ownership: you’ll be expected to figure things out, make things happen, and bring the level of creative ambition the business needs. The opportunity is bigger than cards. The question is: how do we make thoughtfulness, memory, and showing up for your people feel alive, social, desirable, and worth talking about? What success looks like In the first 6 months: - More people know what Escargot is. - More people are talking about Escargot organically. - Organic traffic is growing. - More people are sending cards. - We’re taking bigger, smarter creative swings. - We have repeatable content formats and growth experiments. - We understand which messages, moments, audiences, and channels create energy. - Escargot starts to feel like something people are discovering, sharing, and rooting for.
